title: Keyboard Walk
subtitle: Every Normal-mode key, organized by physical key location.
summary: A row-by-row tour of the QWERTY keyboard mapping each key to its primary Normal-mode function. Useful as a wall poster.

blocks.text: Vim's bindings cover the entire keyboard. This appendix walks the QWERTY layout row-by-row, showing the *primary* Normal-mode function of each key. (Many keys also serve as second halves of operator-motion sequences — see [`appendix.complete-key-reference`](#appendix.complete-key-reference).)

rows: Re-indent operator (== / =motion) / next line's first non-blank

blocks.text:
Most uppercase keys are *bigger* or *opposite* versions of their lowercase counterparts:

  • {key:I} / {key:A} — Insert at line start / Append at line end (vs. cursor position).
  • {key:O} — Open line above (vs. below).
  • {key:E}/{key:W}/{key:B} — WORD-flavored versions of {key:e}/{key:w}/{key:b}.
  • {key:F}/{key:T} — backward versions of {key:f}/{key:t}.
  • {key:N} — previous search match.
  • {key:G} — go to last line (vs. {key:g}{key:g} go to first).
